meta_title: For Founders: Antigravity project template to Solve no portfolio (Intermediate Comparison)
meta_description: Learn how to Antigravity project template and overcome no portfolio. A Intermediate-level Comparison using Antigravity and Claude Code.

content.seo_title: For Founders: Antigravity project template to Solve no portfolio
content.seo_description: Learn how to use an Antigravity project template to overcome the 'no portfolio' trap. An intermediate guide for founders using Antigravity and Claude Code.
content.answer_block: The 'no portfolio' trap kills SaaS momentum. You don't need years of freelance work; you need a high-velocity Antigravity project template. By leveraging modular architecture and Claude Code, you can deploy a production-ready, revenue-generating MVP in hours, turning your technical capability into immediate, verifiable market proof.
content.intro:
The silence of an empty portfolio is the loudest noise in a founder's career. You have the vision, the drive, and the technical aptitude, but the market only trusts what it can touch. You are stuck in the paradox of needing clients to prove your worth, yet needing proof to win your first clients. This isn't a failure of skill; it is a failure of velocity.

Enter the Antigravity project template. This is not just boilerplate code; it is a strategic scaffold designed to compress the development lifecycle. By pairing this architecture with the raw power of Claude Code, you transform from a struggling developer into a high-speed product architect, shipping complex SaaS features before your competitors have even finished their README files.

steps.title: Define the Business Value Proposition
steps.description: Before writing code, define the specific problem your MVP solves to ensure your portfolio piece actually converts users into leads.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Create a project architecture for a SaaS MVP focused on [Insert Niche]. Include a high-conversion landing page, user auth, and a Stripe checkout integration.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'initialize project with Antigravity boilerplate for SaaS architecture'

steps.title: Bootstrap the Database Schema
steps.description: Establish a scalable database foundation that handles multi-tenancy from day one, proving your ability to handle enterprise-grade data structures.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Generate a PostgreSQL schema for a multi-tenant SaaS application, including tables for organizations, users, subscriptions, and usage analytics.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'apply schema migration to local development environment'

steps.title: Implement Modular Core Features
steps.description: Use the Antigravity template to scaffold modular services, ensuring your code is clean, testable, and demonstrates architectural maturity.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Scaffold a modular service layer for user management and subscription billing using the Antigravity pattern.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'generate service modules for authentication and billing'

steps.title: Integrate High-Velocity UI Components
steps.description: Inject polished UI components that mirror modern SaaS standards, proving you can build products that people actually want to use.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Integrate responsive dashboard components for the project using the existing Antigravity UI library standards.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'add responsive dashboard view with Tailwind CSS'

steps.title: Deploy and Validate
steps.description: Automate the CI/CD pipeline to ensure your portfolio project is live, accessible, and ready for public scrutiny.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Configure a GitHub Actions workflow for the Antigravity project to automate testing and deployment to Vercel.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'deploy current build to production environment'

pro_tips: Never build generic clones; focus your portfolio on a specific, painful B2B problem that demonstrates 'domain expertise' to potential clients.
Use Claude Code to document your architectural decisions inside the repo; potential clients love seeing the 'why' behind your code.
Implement a 'Usage Analytics' dashboard in your portfolio project; it proves you understand that SaaS is about metrics, not just features.
Keep your repository public but include a 'Why this matters' section in the README that explicitly calls out the business ROI of your code.
Don't aim for perfection; ship a 'Version 1.0' that works perfectly and iterate based on feedback to show you can manage a product roadmap.
content.next_steps: Once your project is live, write a technical 'Post-Mortem' blog post detailing the challenges you solved and link it directly from your portfolio landing page.
content.conversion_block: You now have the framework to stop talking about your potential and start showing your results. Stop waiting for permission to build and start commanding the market with a professional, high-velocity portfolio. Ready to build? Join our AI Builder community → Start your first project today.
